g.title: Promise Theory has nothing a priori to do with "promises" in programming languages like Javascript, though programming promise functionality is compatible with the Promise Theory definition..

Some might call promises "rules", but they are only self-imposed rules on the agents. · The agents can only promise its own intent to maintain state. If the operating sytem blocks an agent, or some other process interferes then even though it can keep its own promise to try and repair a deviant state, it can't promise that the system will end up according to specification.
